title: Hiyori and Yasuie Reveal Wano’s Pain
description: On pages 10–13, this part focuses on Luffy, Unknown person #9, Hyogoro, Unknown person #10, Unknown person #11, Unknown person #12 as Hiyori’s identity and Yasuie’s sacrifice pull the Wano rebellion into a deeper emotional crisis.
scene: Zoro protects Toko and meets Hiyori, while Yasuie prepares a sacrifice meant to rescue the rebellion’s plan.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The thing you all seem to call Haki

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't force it! Allow the Haki in the parts of your body

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Where it is not needed to flow into your fist!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: 01, 01! You're beating too many of 'em, you idiot!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Damn it! So this is going to continue tomorrow?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm so hungry... I hope they feed us soon!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Or I'll be a Mummy by tomorrow!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Can I ask you something, straw hat?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Why do you wish to fight Kaido?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I heard you already lost once in Kuri.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hmm... with these guards around, I can't really talk about the others.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: D'ya know who the yonko are?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They're the four big-time pirates known as the emperors of the sea. Kaido's one of 'em.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm the guy that's gonna be pirate King, so I've gotta beat 'em all!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: quite a long time ago... he landed here.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Ah... Kinemon mentioned that, now that I think of it.

dialogue.speaker: Kinemon
dialogue.text: Kinemon?! How do you know that name?!

summary: This section advances the Wano - Hiyori and Yasuie storyline with 41 dialogue beats. Hiyori and Yasuie bring the rebellion’s emotional history into the foreground.
